title: Phonology Inventory
description: Schema for inventory JSON files consumed by the phonology-engine package. This is a declarative companion to the runtime validation in Inventory.parse; the parser enforces additional constraints (NFC normalization of segment names, duplicate-key detection, IPA-confusable advisories) that JSON Schema cannot express directly.

name.description: Human-readable inventory name shown in dropdowns and tab titles.

metadata.description: Free-form metadata. The browser-app inventory picker reads metadata.name as the dropdown label; everything else is pass-through.

features.description: Declared distinctive-feature names. Order is the canonical display order. Every segment's bundle is keyed by these names; segments may omit features (the engine defaults missing keys to '0').

items.description: Feature name. Convention: PascalCase or UPPERCASE (e.g. Voice, Nasal, CORONAL, DORSAL).

segments.description: Map of segment symbol (IPA or other) to a feature bundle. Keys are NFC-normalized at parse time. Values within each bundle must be '+', '-', or '0'. Missing features in a bundle are treated as '0' by the engine.
